commit.message:
fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add SetEnvKeyReplacer so nested APP_* env vars are read correctly

Viper's AutomaticEnv() upper-cases keys as-is, producing APP_DATABASE.HOST
(with a literal dot) instead of APP_DATABASE_HOST. Without a replacer, the
env var lookup always fails and the default value (localhost) is used.

Fix: v.SetEnvKeyReplacer(strings.NewReplacer(".", "_"))

Also adds config_test.go with tests for env-var-only and default-value loading.

Co-authored-by: luoxiaojun1992 <6905700+luoxiaojun1992@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: scan report panel destroyed by updateLastScan textContent

Root cause: updateLastScan() used el.textContent which replaced ALL
child content including the scan-report-panel div. Now uses a text
node to update only the label text while preserving child elements.

Verified with Playwright: 14/14 tests pass — panel opens on click,
shows all 4 phases with status icons, task counts, retry info, error
details, duration, and warning indicator. Closes on second click.

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: per-subscription push visibility filtering

Previously, push notifications used a global visibility  if ANYcheck
connected client tab was visible, no push was sent to any device. This
meant a visible desktop tab suppressed phone notifications.

Now each push subscription endpoint is associated with its client IDs.
sendToAll skips only endpoints that have at least one visible client.
Endpoints with no visible client (or no connected client) receive push
as expected.

- Add registerClient(clientId, endpoint) to PushService
- Add isEndpointVisible() for per-endpoint visibility check
- Move visibility filtering from maybeNotify into sendToAll
- Frontend sends clientId on /api/push/subscribe
- New /api/push/register-client endpoint for SSE reconnect
- Frontend auto-registers push endpoint on each SSE connect

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: scan report panel clickability + scan retry on timeout

Two fixes:

1. Scan Report Panel was not opening on click:

   - Added event.stopPropagation() to prevent parent click interference
   - Fixed close-on-outside-click to check parent container

2. Scan Phase 1 timeout now retries with reduced context:
   - On first timeout (180s), creates fresh SDK session
   - Reduces task context from 80 to 20 tasks
   - Reduces scan range from N to min(N, 2) days
   - Logs both attempts with timing for diagnostics
   - If both fail, returns 502 with descriptive error

feat: expandable scan report panel with per-phase metrics

Clicking 'Last scan' now opens a detailed report panel showing:
- Phase 1 (Discovery): success/failure, items found/updated/skipped
- Phase 2 (Enrichment): tasks processed, success/failed/retried counts
- Phase 3 (Update Check): tasks checked, success/failed/retried counts
- Phase 4 (Consolidation): merge suggestions found
- Per-phase timing (duration in seconds)
- Error details for failed tasks (up to 5 shown)
- Warning indicator on 'Last scan' label if any phase had issues

Data collected in real-time during scan execution and persisted in
localStorage. Report survives page refreshes. Closes on click outside.

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: drop session-specific events during mid-switch null window

When switching sessions, state.sessionId is briefly null while the
async history load completes. The session filter in handleEvent
short-circuited on null, letting old-session SSE events render into
the new session DOM. They disappeared on refresh since they were
never persisted for the new session.

Fix: when state.sessionId is null, drop all events that carry a
sessionId (session-specific) except session_created/session_deleted.
Events without sessionId (e.g. connected) still pass through.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: live agent status on task cards + reverse-chronological details

UX improvements:
- Frozen task cards now show context-specific status instead of static
  'Agent working...' — e.g. '🤔 Analyzing request...', '📤 Searching
  emails & Teams...', '🔄 Updating task...', '✅ 3 result(s) found'
- New updateFrozenStatus() for live badge updates without re-render
- freezeTask() accepts optional statusText parameter
- renderDetails() now shows newest entries first (reverse-chronological)
  matching renderConversations() behavior
- Updated CHANGELOG with v2.8.0 entry

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent duplicate thinking blocks on reconnect during active streaming

When a client reconnects while the agent is mid-thinking, the events API
now flushes pending thinking/assistant buffers into the event list and
returns streaming flags. The frontend uses these flags to prime the last
replayed element as a live-streaming element, and the drain queue skips
thought_chunk/message_chunk events whose content is already captured.

Backend: flush unflushed buffers in GET /events, return {events, streaming}
envelope. Frontend: normalizeEventsResponse handles both old array and new
envelope formats. primeStreamingState converts the last replayed
thinking/assistant element into a live one. isDuplicateOfReplay now skips
streaming chunks when the element is already primed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: switch from Claude Opus 4.6 to default model for faster responses

Removed explicit 'claude-opus-4-6' model override from:
- Intent classification (analyzePrompt session)
- Correction verification session

This significantly reduces response time:
- Before (Opus): 26-39s per intent classification
- After (default): 12-19s per intent classification

Process isolation verified: 3 concurrent requests complete
independently with no data loss or interference.

commit.message:
Add SystemVerilog testbenches for RTL primitives library

Create 16 standalone testbench files in testbenches/ directory covering:
- CDC: sync_2ff, async_fifo
- Arbiters: fixed_priority_arbiter, round_robin_arbiter
- Bitops: priority_encoder, popcount, barrel_shifter
- Counters: up_counter, gray_counter
- Memory: dual_port_ram, simple_fifo
- Handshake: skid_buffer
- Pipeline: pipeline_register, delay_line
- Encoding: gray_encoder
- Clock: clock_divider

Each testbench uses `timescale 1ns/1ps, waveform dumps, $error for
failures, PASS/FAIL summary, and tests basic functionality plus corner
cases such as reset, overflow, backpressure, and one-bit Gray transitions.
Testbenches assume DUT modules are compiled separately in the same
compilation unit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add pipeline primitives directory with 6 RTL modules

- pipeline_register: single stage with enable/flush
- delay_line: parameterized shift register with per-tap outputs
- latency_balancer: aligns two parallel paths via delay compensation
- pipeline_fork: 1-to-N valid/ready fork with completion mask
- pipeline_join: N-to-1 all-valid gate join
- pipeline_bubble_injector: NOP insertion / bubble injection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add memory RTL primitives: dual_port_ram, simple_fifo, register_file, cam, line_buffer, circular_buffer

- dual_port_ram: true dual-port RAM with independent clocks, registered-read for block RAM inference
- simple_fifo: synchronous FIFO with explicit count register for full/empty detection
- register_file: multi-read-port file with combinational reads and write-to-read forwarding
- content_addressable_memory: fully-associative CAM with priority-encoded hit address
- line_buffer: streaming image line buffer outputting a vertical column of NUM_LINES pixels
- circular_buffer: ring buffer with head/tail pointers, full/empty, and count output

All modules: snake_case signals, UPPER_CASE parameters, always_ff/always_comb,
no initial blocks, no vendor primitives, fully synthesizable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: rtl-primitives
repository.description: Open Source RTL primitives library for FPGA and ASIC designers in SystemVerilog

commit.message:
Add counters/ directory with 8 synthesizable SystemVerilog primitives

- up_counter: synchronous up counter with enable, load, registered overflow
- down_counter: synchronous down counter with enable, load, registered underflow
- up_down_counter: bidirectional counter with overflow/underflow detection
- saturating_counter: clamps at MAX/MIN with combinational at_max/at_min flags
- gray_counter: binary counter with XOR Gray-code output for CDC use
- event_counter: event accumulator with threshold comparison and sync clear
- watchdog_timer: programmable down-counter watchdog with one-cycle timeout pulse
- programmable_interval_timer: configurable periodic/one-shot tick generator

All modules follow project conventions: snake_case signals, UPPER_CASE
parameters, always_ff/always_comb, active-low synchronous reset (rst_n),
no initial blocks, no vendor-specific primitives.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: rtl-primitives
repository.description: Open Source RTL primitives library for FPGA and ASIC designers in SystemVerilog

commit.message:
Add bitops/ RTL primitive modules

Add 9 fully-synthesizable combinational SystemVerilog modules:
- priority_encoder: lowest-index set bit to binary with valid flag
- leading_zero_counter: MSB leading zero count, width clog2(WIDTH+1)
- trailing_zero_counter: LSB trailing zero count, width clog2(WIDTH+1)
- popcount: adder-tree population count, O(log2 WIDTH) depth
- onehot_encoder: one-hot to binary via OR reduction
- onehot_decoder: binary to one-hot with bounds check
- thermometer_encoder: binary N to N-ones thermometer code
- barrel_shifter: logical/arithmetic left+right, cascaded-mux
- rotate_unit: left/right rotation, cascaded-mux with right->left conversion

All modules verified with pyslang 10.0.0 elaboration: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
arbiters: add 7 synthesizable SystemVerilog arbiter primitives

- fixed_priority_arbiter: combinational, isolate-lowest-bit trick
- round_robin_arbiter: masked-priority rotating, registered output
- weighted_round_robin_arbiter: per-port credit counter, programmable weight
- masked_priority_arbiter: combinational with external disable mask + fallback
- tree_arbiter: binary tree O(log N) depth, generate/assign for clean linting
- fair_rotating_arbiter: one-hot ptr advances every grant cycle
- parking_arbiter: sticky grant held while owner's request is asserted

All modules are fully synthesizable, vendor-primitive-free, and pass
Verilator --Wall lint with no warnings.

commit.message:
Add handshake RTL primitives (7 SystemVerilog modules)

Introduces a new handshake/ directory with 7 fully synthesizable
SystemVerilog modules implementing common ready/valid handshake patterns:

- ready_valid_stage: single registered pipeline stage with back-pressure
- skid_buffer: 2-entry elastic buffer with registered in_ready
- elastic_buffer: circular FIFO (power-of-2 depth) with combinational head output
- mailbox_fifo: single-entry mailbox supporting simultaneous wr+rd
- credit_flow_controller: sender-side credit counter with registered output
- pipeline_register_slice: fully registered slice (EMPTY/FULL/BOTH FSM)
- backpressure_adapter: absorbs always-valid source into FIFO with overflow flag

All modules follow project conventions: snake_case ports/signals,
UPPER_CASE parameters, always_ff for flip-flops, active-low rst_n,
no initial blocks, no vendor primitives, no combinational ready/valid loops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add reset primitives library (6 synthesizable SV modules)

- reset_synchronizer: async-assert/sync-deassert 2-FF chain
- reset_stretcher: counter-based minimum pulse width enforcement
- reset_pulse_generator: edge-detected synchronous pulse generator
- power_on_reset: shift-register POR with async preset technique
- reset_bridge: cross-domain reset synchronizer
- reset_controller: multi-domain sequenced reset with staggered release

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: rtl-primitives
repository.description: Open Source RTL primitives library for FPGA and ASIC designers in SystemVerilog

commit.message:
Fix toggle_synchronizer: separate async_reg attribute per sync stage

Declare stage1 and stage2 on separate lines each with their own
(* DONT_TOUCH *) (* async_reg *) attributes so synthesis tools
apply placement constraints correctly per individual register.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: rtl-primitives
repository.description: Open Source RTL primitives library for FPGA and ASIC designers in SystemVerilog

commit.message:
Add CDC primitives library (8 modules)

Add a complete set of synthesizable SystemVerilog CDC primitives:

- sync_2ff: 2-flop metastability synchronizer with DONT_TOUCH/async_reg
- sync_3ff: 3-flop synchronizer for higher-MTBF applications
- pulse_synchronizer: toggle-detect single-cycle pulse CDC
- toggle_synchronizer: multi-bit level/Gray-code bus synchronizer
- req_ack_synchronizer: 4-phase handshake CDC with src_ack feedback
- bundled_data_synchronizer: req/ack-guarded multi-bit data transfer
- gray_pointer_sync: Gray-code pointer 2-FF synchronizer (async FIFO use)
- async_fifo: dual-clock FIFO with Gray-code pointers and full/empty flags

All modules use always_ff, active-low resets (rst_n), snake_case signals,
UPPER_CASE parameters, and include full header documentation with usage
notes and example instantiations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: kill existing server before restart in START-AGENT-ZERO.bat

Previously the launcher just opened the browser if port 3000 was busy,
leaving the old server running. Now it finds the PID on port 3000,
terminates it, waits 2 seconds, then starts a fresh server.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: intelligent duplicate detection with subject normalization and task reactivation

Problem: Scan created duplicate tasks for the same email thread when
subject prefixes differed (e.g. 'Re: RG Netzwerkprüfung' vs
'[EXTERN] RG Netzwerkprüfung'). Done tasks were only skipped, never
reactivated when new activity appeared.

Changes:
- Add stripSubjectPrefixes() to remove Re:, Fw:, AW:, WG:, [EXTERN],
  [EXTERNAL] etc. before comparison
- normalizeForCompare() now preserves German umlauts (äöüß) and strips
  prefixes before lowercasing
- isSimilarTitle() uses dual threshold: Jaccard >0.6 OR subset ratio
  >=0.9 (catches prefix-only differences)
- Safety-net now reactivates done tasks instead of creating duplicates:
  sets status to needs-attention, adds reactivation history entry,
  resets enrichment for fresh content extraction
- Backward-compat dedup uses normalized comparison instead of exact match
- AI scan prompt explicitly instructs to ignore subject prefixes
- Add session lifecycle management and orphan subprocess reaper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: decouple ACP resume from GET so session switch never blocks

Problem: GET /api/sessions/:id blocked on bridge.loadSession() (ACP
context replay). For large sessions this timed out, causing "switch
session failed" while partial history was already in the DOM and the
old session title/state stayed visible.

Changes:
- session-manager: add ensureResumed() with dedup for concurrent calls
- routes: GET returns stored metadata immediately, fires resume in
  background; POST messages awaits ensureResumed before prompting
- state: resetSessionUI() now clears title, cwd, configOptions
- commands: switch error handlers call resetSessionUI() to clean up
  partial state instead of just showing the error message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[master] pfcwd: refactor LAG config handling into complete resource manager fixture (#22903)

* fix(pfcwd): refactor LAG config handling into complete resource manager fixture

Replace the teardown-only restore_original_config fixture with manage_lag_config,
a complete resource manager that:
- Encapsulates both __shutdown_lag_members (setup) and __restore_original_config
  (teardown) in a single fixture using the factory/callable pattern
- Guarantees config restoration even on test failure
- Keeps the test function clean of explicit backup/restore calls

Addresses review feedback: a fixture should be a complete resource manager
(backup + restore), not just a teardown hook.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sonic-mgmt
repository.description: Configuration management examples for SONiC

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update backend/controllers/communityController.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix test_add_rack flakiness by waiting for BGP convergence before DB comparison (#22895)

* Fix test_add_rack flakiness by waiting for BGP convergence before DB comparison

The test_add_rack test was failing ~1% of runs with 'DB compare failed
after adding T0 via generic patch updater'. Root cause: DB comparison
ran before BGP sessions established, causing app-db route entry
mismatches.

Changes:
- Add is_bgp_session_established() helper that returns bool for use
  with wait_until retry mechanism
- Move BGP session convergence check BEFORE DB comparison in
  generic_patch_add_t0(), so app-db routes are populated before
  comparing against baseline
- BGP check now retries with wait_until instead of bare assert

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Storm Liang <stormliang@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
ansible: support pulling ceos_image from docker registry (#22794)

What is the motivation for this PR?
In environments that enforce registry-only container image policies (e.g. Microsoft requiring all images to originate from ACR or MCR), the previous approach of building ceos_image locally or re-tagging a pulled image with a local name would trigger security alerts. This PR allows ceos_image to be sourced directly from a user-configured registry, so containers always reference the registry-prefixed image name and no locally-built alias is created.

How did you do it?
Three files are changed:

ansible/group_vars/vm_host/ceos.yml

Added three commented-out placeholder variables: ceos_registry, ceos_registry_username, ceos_registry_password.
When ceos_registry is uncommented and set, the new registry logic is activated. Credentials are optional and only needed for authenticated registries.
ansible/roles/vm_set/tasks/add_ceos_list.yml

When ceos_registry is defined, check whether <registry>/<ceos_image> is already cached locally (a prior pull). If found, it is used directly — no docker tag is performed, so no local alias is created.
If not cached and ceos_registry is defined, optionally log in (only when credentials are provided) and docker pull the image.
Only falls back to the existing download-ceos_image_orig-and-build path when registry is not configured or the pull fails.
ceos_image_found logic is registry-aware: when ceos_registry is defined, a plain local ceos_image does not count as "found", ensuring the registry path is always taken.
ansible/roles/eos/tasks/ceos.yml and ceos_ensure_reachable.yml

After loading group_vars/vm_host/ceos.yml, a docker_image_info check determines whether the registry image is available locally.
ceos_effective_image is set to <registry>/<ceos_image> when the registry image is present, or plain ceos_image otherwise.
All docker_container tasks use ceos_effective_image instead of ceos_image, so containers always reference the registry image in registry-enabled environments.
How did you verify/test it?
Tested "add-topo" by specifying ceos_registry. The ceos image from the registry is used successfully.
Tested "add-topo" without specifying ceos_registry. The existing logic of using a local ceos image or preparing ceos image from ceos_image_orig works as expected.

Signed-off-by: Xin Wang <xiwang5@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: re-render detail panel after unfreezeTask to reflect changes

unfreezeTask() previously only removed the frozen CSS class but did not
re-render the task card or detail panel. This caused a bug where
immediate intents (update/summarize/rename/answer) processed the data
correctly on the server but the UI never showed the changes — the user
had to press F5.

Root cause: fetchTasks() runs while the task is still frozen, so
renderTasks() correctly skips the frozen task's detail panel. But after
unfreezeTask() in the finally block, no re-render was triggered.

The scan process already solved this by calling refreshSingleTask()
after unfreezeTask(). This fix applies the same principle directly in
unfreezeTask() — re-rendering from the existing tasks array (no extra
API call needed).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reduce Lumi response startup latency

Move non-critical send work off the first-token path, parallelize session setup I/O, and defer title generation until the session is idle.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve UX design issues across multiple pages

- Fix TOAST_REMOVE_DELAY from 1000000ms (16min bug) to 5000ms
- Increase TOAST_LIMIT from 1 to 3 for concurrent feedback
- Add success/error feedback + loading state to webhook test button
- Standardize loading states: replace plain text with Spinner component
  in approvals, audit-logs, webhooks, and templates pages
- Replace title attrs with proper Tooltip components on icon-only buttons
  in webhooks and templates pages
- Improve stale search results indicator: use grayscale instead of
  opacity-60 for better accessibility in global search

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(posts): Add pending tasks for dawn-ai config and code review

Add two new todo items to the TODO post:
- Configure dawn-ai and test interface connectivity
- Review code

Also update the post's 'updated' timestamp to 2026-03-13.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Batch replay DOM operations to reduce reflow

During history replay, rendering was the dominant bottleneck (~4s of 5.5s
total page load) because each of potentially thousands of events triggered
individual appendChild + scrollToBottom (which forces layout recalc).

Fix: Render replay events into an offscreen DocumentFragment, then append
to dom.messages in a single operation with display:none to skip layout.

- Add state.replayTarget for fragment-based rendering during replay
- appendMessageElement() skips scroll/reflow when replayTarget is set
- loadHistory(): fragment + display:none/show around batch append
- loadNewEvents(): fragment for incremental sync events
- replayById()/replayQuery() search fragment then live DOM for
  tool_call_update, permission_response, bash_result lookups

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add `export-audit` CLI command for wallet transaction CSV export (#7700)

Wallet needs to export transactions as a CSV for auditing, with optional
date range filtering and fiat conversion.

## Changes

### `cli.rs`
- New `ExportAuditArgs` struct with: `--output-file`, `--start-date`,
`--end-date`, `--conversion-rate`, `--currency` (default: `USD`)
- New `ExportAudit(ExportAuditArgs)` variant in `CliCommands`
- `parse_utc_datetime` helper accepting RFC3339 and `YYYY-MM-DD` /
`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` formats

### `commands.rs`
- `ExportAudit` handler: fetches all completed transactions, filters by
date range, calls `write_audit_to_csv_file`
- `write_audit_to_csv_file`: emits CSV with columns matching the spec —
transaction type is `Deposit` (inbound/coinbase) or `Withdraw`
(outbound); transaction hash derived from first kernel hash; amounts in
XTM (Minotari); optional fiat column using supplied rate

### `wallet_modes.rs`
- Added `ExportAudit` arm to the exhaustive command-match test

## Example

```bash
minotari_console_wallet export-audit \
  --output-file audit.csv \
  --start-date 2021-01-01 \
  --end-date 2021-12-31 \
  --conversion-rate 3130.0 \
  --currency USD
```

Output CSV columns:
```
ID, Transaction Hash, Status, Transaction Type, DateTime (UTC),
From Address, To Address, Amount, AmountTicker, Amount (USD),
Txn Fee, FeeTicker, Fee (USD)
```

commit.message:
Add API spec and client architecture documentation

- docs/api.md: Complete REST + SSE API reference with all endpoints,
  parameters, responses, SSE event types, and implementation details.
  References Zod schema/type names for grep-traceable code links.doc

- docs/client-architecture.md: Frontend architecture covering module
  structure, boot sequence, connection lifecycle, parallel page loading,
  event handling (live vs replay), session management, and key design
  decisions.

- test/doc-coverage.test.ts: Staleness guard that extracts endpoint
  paths from routes.ts and asserts they appear in docs/api.md. CI fails
  if a route is added without updating documentation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
